message:
✨ Recreate 15 CNCF install missions with verified install methods (#1461)

Each mission rebuilt from official project documentation with
verified URLs (all return HTTP 200).

Projects: cartography, chaosblade, devfile, kgateway, kpt,
kuasar, metal3-io, oras, ovn-kubernetes, schemahero,
slimtoolkit, submariner, virtual-kubelet, vitess, runme-notebooks

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-cartography",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Cartography on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Cartography is a Python tool that consolidates infrastructure assets and the relationships between them in an intuitive graph view powered by a Neo4j database. It helps security teams understand risk exposure by mapping dependencies across cloud providers, SaaS platforms, and Kubernetes clusters.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy Neo4j as a prerequisite",
+        "description": "Cartography requires a Neo4j database. Deploy Neo4j using its official Helm chart.\n```bash\nhelm repo add neo4j https://helm.neo4j.com/neo4j\nhelm repo update\nhelm install neo4j neo4j/neo4j --namespace cartography --create-namespace --set neo4j.password=changeme\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Cartography via pip",
+        "description": "Install the Cartography CLI tool using pip. This is the official installation method.\n```bash\npip install cartography\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Run Cartography as a Kubernetes Job",
+        "description": "Create a Kubernetes Job that runs Cartography to sync infrastructure data into Neo4j.\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: batch/v1\nkind: Job\nmetadata:\n  name: cartography-sync\n  namespace: cartography\nspec:\n  template:\n    spec:\n      containers:\n      - name: cartography\n        image: ghcr.io/cartography-cncf/cartography:latest\n        command: [\"cartography\"]\n        args:\n        - \"--neo4j-uri\"\n        - \"bolt://neo4j.cartography.svc.cluster.local:7687\"\n        - \"--neo4j-password-env-var\"\n        - \"NEO4J_PASSWORD\"\n        env:\n        - name: NEO4J_PASSWORD\n          value: \"changeme\"\n      restartPolicy: Never\n  backoffLimit: 3\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the deployment",
+        "description": "Check that Neo4j is running and the Cartography job completed successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace cartography\nkubectl get jobs --namespace cartography\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will have Neo4j running in the cartography namespace and the Cartography sync job completed. You can then query Neo4j to explore your infrastructure graph.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add neo4j https://helm.neo4j.com/neo4j\nhelm repo update\nhelm install neo4j neo4j/neo4j --namespace cartography --create-namespace --set neo4j.password=changeme",
+        "pip install cartography",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace cartography"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Cartography Job",
+        "description": "Delete the Cartography sync job from the cartography namespace."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall Neo4j",
+        "description": "Remove the Neo4j Helm release and clean up persistent volumes."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the namespace",
+        "description": "Remove the cartography namespace and verify all resources are cleaned up."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Cartography",
+        "description": "Update the pip package to the latest version and re-run the sync job with the new container image."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Neo4j",
+        "description": "Update the Neo4j Helm release to a newer chart version."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Cartography job fails to connect to Neo4j",
+        "description": "Ensure the Neo4j service is running and the bolt URI is correct.\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace cartography\nkubectl logs job/cartography-sync --namespace cartography\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Authentication errors",
+        "description": "Verify the Neo4j password matches between the Helm chart values and the Cartography job environment variable."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "security",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "cartography"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Job",
+      "Namespace",
+      "Secret"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "0.133.0",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "ghcr.io/cartography-cncf/cartography:latest"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/install.html",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/cartography-cncf/cartography"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl",
+      "pip"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm, kubectl, and pip installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-18T18:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "manual-audit",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-chaosblade",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure ChaosBlade on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "ChaosBlade is an Alibaba open source chaos engineering tool that follows chaos engineering principles to help enterprises improve the fault tolerance of distributed systems. The chaosblade-operator enables Kubernetes-native chaos experiments defined as CRDs.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Download the ChaosBlade CLI",
+        "description": "Download the ChaosBlade CLI toolkit from the official releases.\n```bash\ncurl -fsSL https://github.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade/releases/download/v1.8.0/chaosblade-1.8.0-linux_amd64.tar.gz -o chaosblade.tar.gz\ntar -xzf chaosblade.tar.gz\nsudo mv chaosblade-1.8.0 /opt/chaosblade\nexport PATH=$PATH:/opt/chaosblade\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install the ChaosBlade Operator using Helm",
+        "description": "Clone the operator repo and install it via Helm to enable Kubernetes-native chaos experiments.\n```bash\nkubectl create ns chaosblade\ngit clone https://github.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade-operator.git\ncd chaosblade-operator/deploy/helm\nhelm package ./chaosblade-operator\nhelm install chaosblade chaosblade-operator-*.tgz --namespace chaosblade\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the operator installation",
+        "description": "Check that the ChaosBlade operator pods are running in the chaosblade namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace chaosblade\nkubectl get crd | grep chaosblade\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Run a sample chaos experiment",
+        "description": "Create a sample ChaosBlade experiment to verify everything works.\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: chaosblade.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ChaosBlade\nmetadata:\n  name: cpu-load-test\nspec:\n  experiments:\n  - scope: pod\n    target: cpu\n    action: fullload\n    desc: \"CPU full load test\"\n    matchers:\n    - name: names\n      value:\n      - \"your-pod-name\"\n    - name: namespace\n      value:\n      - \"default\"\n    - name: cpu-percent\n      value:\n      - \"50\"\nEOF\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show the ChaosBlade operator running in the chaosblade namespace with the ChaosBlade CRDs registered. You can then define chaos experiments as Kubernetes resources.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl create ns chaosblade",
+        "helm install chaosblade chaosblade-operator-*.tgz --namespace chaosblade",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace chaosblade",
+        "kubectl get crd | grep chaosblade"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove all chaos experiments",
+        "description": "Delete all ChaosBlade experiment resources before uninstalling the operator."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall the Helm release",
+        "description": "Remove the chaosblade-operator Helm release from the chaosblade namespace."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up CRDs and namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the ChaosBlade CRDs and the chaosblade namespace."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Download the latest CLI",
+        "description": "Download the latest ChaosBlade CLI release from GitHub."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the operator",
+        "description": "Rebuild the Helm chart from the latest operator source and run helm upgrade."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Operator pods not starting",
+        "description": "Check the operator logs for errors.\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app=chaosblade-operator --namespace chaosblade\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Chaos experiment stuck in pending",
+        "description": "Verify the target pod exists and the namespace matches the experiment spec.\n```bash\nkubectl describe chaosblade cpu-load-test\n```"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "chaos-engineering",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "chaosblade"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Namespace",
+      "Pod",
+      "CustomResourceDefinition"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v1.8.0",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "chaosbladeio/chaosblade-operator:v1.8.0"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://chaosblade-io.gitbook.io/chaosblade-help-zh-cn/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl",
+      "git"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm, kubectl, and git installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-18T18:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "manual-audit",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-devfile",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Devfile Registry on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Devfile provides a Kube-native API for cloud development workspaces. The Devfile Registry hosts reusable devfile stacks that define development environments. This mission deploys a self-hosted Devfile Registry on Kubernetes using the official Helm chart.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Clone the registry-support repository",
+        "description": "Clone the devfile registry-support repository which contains the Helm chart for deploying a Devfile Registry.\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/devfile/registry-support.git\ncd registry-support\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Devfile Registry using Helm",
+        "description": "Deploy the Devfile Registry using the included Helm chart.\n```bash\nhelm install devfile-registry deploy/chart/devfile-registry --namespace devfile --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the Devfile Registry pods are running.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace devfile\nkubectl get svc --namespace devfile\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Test the registry API",
+        "description": "Verify the registry is serving devfile stacks by querying its API.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/devfile-registry 8080:8080 --namespace devfile &\ncurl http://localhost:8080/v2index\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show the Devfile Registry pods running in the devfile namespace. The registry serves devfile stacks via its REST API, allowing developers to discover and use pre-built development environment configurations.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "git clone https://github.com/devfile/registry-support.git",
+        "helm install devfile-registry deploy/chart/devfile-registry --namespace devfile --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace devfile"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall the Helm release",
+        "description": "Remove the devfile-registry Helm release from the devfile namespace."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the namespace",
+        "description": "Remove the devfile namespace and all associated resources."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pull latest registry-support",
+        "description": "Update the registry-support repository to get the latest Helm chart."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Run helm upgrade",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Devfile Registry deployment with the updated chart."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Registry pods not starting",
+        "description": "Check pod logs for errors and ensure container images can be pulled.\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app=devfile-registry --namespace devfile\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Registry returns empty index",
+        "description": "Verify the registry was built with devfile stacks included. Check the registry-support build documentation for how to include custom stacks."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "developer-tools",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "devfile"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Service",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v1.2.1",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "quay.io/devfile/devfile-index:next"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://devfile.io/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/devfile/api"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",

+      "git"
+    ],

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-kgateway",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure kgateway on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "kgateway is a resilient, performance-oriented control plane that implements the Kubernetes Gateway API for Envoy. It is the most widely deployed gateway in Kubernetes for microservices, supporting API gateway, hybrid application migration, and complex tenancy environments.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install Gateway API CRDs",
+        "description": "Install the Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs which kgateway requires.\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install kgateway CRDs via Helm",
+        "description": "Install the kgateway CRDs from the repository's Helm chart.\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/kgateway-dev/kgateway.git\ncd kgateway\nhelm install kgateway-crds install/helm/kgateway-crds --namespace kgateway-system --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install kgateway using Helm",
+        "description": "Deploy kgateway using the Helm chart from the repository.\n```bash\nhelm install kgateway install/helm/kgateway --namespace kgateway-system\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that kgateway pods are running and the Gateway API resources are available.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace kgateway-system\nkubectl get gatewayclass\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create a sample Gateway",
+        "description": "Create a Gateway resource to verify the installation is working.\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1\nkind: Gateway\nmetadata:\n  name: example-gateway\n  namespace: kgateway-system\nspec:\n  gatewayClassName: kgateway\n  listeners:\n  - name: http\n    port: 8080\n    protocol: HTTP\nEOF\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show kgateway pods running in the kgateway-system namespace with the GatewayClass registered. You can then create Gateway and HTTPRoute resources to manage traffic routing via Envoy.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml",
+        "helm install kgateway-crds install/helm/kgateway-crds --namespace kgateway-system --create-namespace",
+        "helm install kgateway install/helm/kgateway --namespace kgateway-system",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace kgateway-system"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove Gateway resources",
+        "description": "Delete all Gateway and HTTPRoute resources before uninstalling kgateway."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall kgateway Helm releases",
+        "description": "Remove the kgateway and kgateway-crds Helm releases."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the kgateway-system namespace and remove Gateway API CRDs if no longer needed."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pull latest source",
+        "description": "Update the kgateway repository to get the latest Helm charts."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade via Helm",
+        "description": "Run helm upgrade for both the CRDs and the main kgateway chart."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "GatewayClass not found",
+        "description": "Ensure the kgateway CRDs were installed before the main chart.\n```bash\nkubectl get crd | grep gateway\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Gateway stuck in pending",
+        "description": "Check the kgateway controller logs for errors.\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway --namespace kgateway-system\n```"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",

+    ],

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-kpt",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure kpt on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "kpt is a package-centric toolchain that enables a WYSIWYG configuration authoring, automation, and delivery experience for Kubernetes. It simplifies managing Kubernetes platforms and KRM-driven infrastructure at scale by treating configuration as data.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install the kpt CLI",
+        "description": "Download and install the kpt CLI binary for your platform.\n```bash\n# Linux amd64\ncurl -fsSL https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.61.1/kpt_linux_amd64 -o kpt\nchmod +x kpt\nsudo mv kpt /usr/local/bin/\n\n# Verify installation\nkpt version\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Initialize a kpt package",
+        "description": "Create a new kpt package or fetch an existing one from a Git repository.\n```bash\nmkdir my-package && cd my-package\nkpt pkg init\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Fetch a sample kpt package",
+        "description": "Fetch a sample package from the kpt repository to explore configuration management.\n```bash\nkpt pkg get https://github.com/kptdev/kpt.git/package-examples/helloworld-set@main\ncat helloworld-set/Kptfile\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy a kpt package to Kubernetes",
+        "description": "Use kpt live to deploy the package to your Kubernetes cluster with resource inventory tracking.\n```bash\nkpt live init helloworld-set\nkpt live apply helloworld-set\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the deployment",
+        "description": "Check the status of the deployed resources.\n```bash\nkpt live status helloworld-set\nkubectl get all\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will have the kpt CLI available and able to fetch, render, and deploy packages to Kubernetes. The live apply feature provides resource inventory tracking and pruning capabilities.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "curl -fsSL https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.61.1/kpt_linux_amd64 -o kpt\nchmod +x kpt\nsudo mv kpt /usr/local/bin/",
+        "kpt pkg get https://github.com/kptdev/kpt.git/package-examples/helloworld-set@main",
+        "kpt live init helloworld-set\nkpt live apply helloworld-set"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove deployed packages",
+        "description": "Use kpt live destroy to remove resources deployed by kpt."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the kpt CLI",
+        "description": "Delete the kpt binary from /usr/local/bin/."

+        "description": "Install the appropriate sandboxer binary for your use case. Kuasar supports MicroVM (Cloud Hypervisor, QEMU, StratoVirt), Wasm (WasmEdge, Wasmtime), and App Kernel (Quark) sandboxers.\n```bash\n# Install the vmm-sandboxer for MicroVM support\nsudo cp vmm-sandboxer /usr/local/bin/\nsudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/vmm-sandboxer\n\n# Or install the wasm-sandboxer for Wasm support\nsudo cp wasm-sandboxer /usr/local/bin/\nsudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/wasm-sandboxer\n```"

+      },
+      {

+        "description": "Use ORAS to pull an OCI artifact back from the registry.\n```bash\nmkdir output && cd output\noras pull localhost:5000/myartifact:v1\ncat artifact.txt\n```"

+        "title": "Deploy the Submariner Broker",

+      },

+        "title": "Join clusters to the Broker",
+        "description": "Join each participating cluster to the Broker. This installs the Submariner components on the cluster.\n```bash\nsubctl join broker-info.subm --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-a/kubeconfig --clusterid cluster-a\nsubctl join broker-info.subm --kubeconfig /path/to/cluster-b/kubeconfig --clusterid cluster-b\n```"

+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Virtual Kubelet on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Virtual Kubelet is an open source Kubernetes kubelet implementation that masquerades as a kubelet to connect Kubernetes to other APIs such as serverless container platforms (ACI, Fargate), IoT Edge, and more. It provides a pluggable provider architecture.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Virtual Kubelet binary",
+        "description": "Build or install the Virtual Kubelet binary with your chosen provider. Virtual Kubelet is a library, so you typically install a provider-specific build.\n```bash\n# Install from source with a provider\ngo install github.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet@v1.12.0\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure the provider",
+        "description": "Create a configuration file for your chosen provider. Each provider has its own config format. Example for a mock provider:\n```bash\ncat <<EOF > vk-config.json\n{\n  \"provider\": \"mock\",\n  \"nodeName\": \"virtual-node\",\n  \"operatingSystem\": \"Linux\"\n}\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy Virtual Kubelet as a Deployment",
+        "description": "Deploy Virtual Kubelet as a Kubernetes Deployment that registers a virtual node.\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: apps/v1\nkind: Deployment\nmetadata:\n  name: virtual-kubelet\n  namespace: kube-system\nspec:\n  replicas: 1\n  selector:\n    matchLabels:\n      app: virtual-kubelet\n  template:\n    metadata:\n      labels:\n        app: virtual-kubelet\n    spec:\n      serviceAccountName: virtual-kubelet\n      containers:\n      - name: virtual-kubelet\n        image: virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet:v1.12.0\n        args:\n        - --provider\n        - mock\n        - --nodename\n        - virtual-node\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create RBAC for Virtual Kubelet",
+        "description": "Create the necessary ServiceAccount and RBAC permissions.\n```bash\nkubectl create serviceaccount virtual-kubelet -n kube-system\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1\nkind: ClusterRoleBinding\nmetadata:\n  name: virtual-kubelet\nsubjects:\n- kind: ServiceAccount\n  name: virtual-kubelet\n  namespace: kube-system\nroleRef:\n  kind: ClusterRole\n  name: cluster-admin\n  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the virtual node",
+        "description": "Check that the virtual node appears in the node list.\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes\nkubectl describe node virtual-node\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-vitess",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Vitess on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Vitess is a cloud-native horizontally-scalable distributed database system built around MySQL. It can achieve unlimited scaling through generalized sharding while keeping application code and queries agnostic to data distribution. The Vitess Operator simplifies deployment on Kubernetes.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Vitess Operator CRDs",
+        "description": "Apply the Vitess Operator manifests which include the CRDs and operator deployment.\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/planetscale/vitess-operator/main/deploy/operator.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the operator is running",
+        "description": "Check that the Vitess Operator pods are running.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -l app=vitess-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy a VitessCluster",
+        "description": "Create a VitessCluster resource to deploy a Vitess cluster. This example deploys a minimal single-shard setup.\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: planetscale.com/v2\nkind: VitessCluster\nmetadata:\n  name: example\nspec:\n  images:\n    vtgate: vitess/lite:v23.0.3\n    vttablet: vitess/lite:v23.0.3\n    vtbackup: vitess/lite:v23.0.3\n    vtctld: vitess/lite:v23.0.3\n    mysqld:\n      mysql80Compatible: vitess/lite:v23.0.3\n  cells:\n  - name: zone1\n    gateway:\n      replicas: 1\n      resources:\n        requests:\n          cpu: 100m\n          memory: 256Mi\n  keyspaces:\n  - name: commerce\n    turndownPolicy: Immediate\n    partitionings:\n    - equal:\n        parts: 1\n        shardTemplate:\n          databaseInitScriptSecret:\n            name: example-cluster-config\n            key: init_db.sql\n          tabletPools:\n          - cell: zone1\n            type: replica\n            replicas: 2\n            mysqld:\n              resources:\n                requests:\n                  cpu: 100m\n                  memory: 256Mi\n            dataVolumeClaimTemplate:\n              accessModes: [\"ReadWriteOnce\"]\n              resources:\n                requests:\n                  storage: 10Gi\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the Vitess cluster",
+        "description": "Check that all Vitess components are running.\n```bash\nkubectl get vitessclusters\nkubectl get pods -l planetscale.com/cluster=example\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Connect to the Vitess cluster",
+        "description": "Port-forward the VTGate service to connect with a MySQL client.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/example-vtgate-zone1 3306:3306 &\nmysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P 3306 -u user\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show the Vitess Operator running and managing VitessCluster resources. VTGate provides MySQL-compatible access to the distributed database, while VTTablet manages the underlying MySQL instances.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/planetscale/vitess-operator/main/deploy/operator.yaml",
+        "kubectl get pods -l app=vitess-operator",
+        "kubectl get vitessclusters"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the VitessCluster",
+        "description": "Delete the VitessCluster resource to remove all Vitess components."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the operator",
+        "description": "Delete the Vitess Operator deployment and CRDs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up persistent volumes",
+        "description": "Delete any PVCs created by the Vitess tablets."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the operator",
+        "description": "Apply the latest operator manifests from the vitess-operator repository."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update Vitess images",
+        "description": "Update the VitessCluster resource with new image versions and apply."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Tablets stuck in NotServing state",
+        "description": "Check tablet logs and ensure the init script ran successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l planetscale.com/component=vttablet\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "VTGate returns connection errors",
+        "description": "Verify that tablets are healthy and the topology is correct.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/example-vtctld 15000:15000\n# Access the Vitess dashboard at http://localhost:15000\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "PVC binding issues",
+        "description": "Ensure a StorageClass is configured and PVs can be dynamically provisioned.\n```bash\nkubectl get sc\nkubectl get pvc\n```"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
